topics.name: App Configurations
topics.description: Every application instance exposes a `config` object that contains the configuration settings for that application. You can modify its properties before mounting your application.

topics.name: Async Components
topics.description: In large applications, we may need to divide the app into smaller chunks and only load a component from the server when it's needed. To make that possible, Vue has a `defineAsyncComponent` function.

topics.name: Attribute Inheritance
topics.description: Attribute inheritance aka "fallthrough attributes" is a feature of Vue.js that allows you to inherit attributes from a parent component.

topics.name: Component Registration
topics.description: A Vue component needs to be "registered" so that Vue knows where to locate its implementation when it is encountered in a template. There are two ways to register components: global and local.

topics.name: Conditional Rendering
topics.description: The directive `v-if` is used to conditionally render a block. The block will only be rendered if the directive's expression returns a truthy value.

topics.name: Create Vue
topics.description: create-vue is a CLI tool that helps you create a new Vue project with a single command. It is a simple and easy-to-use tool that saves you time and effort when setting up a new Vue project.

topics.name: Debugging
topics.description: Debugging in Vue.js involves identifying and fixing issues in your Vue applications. It’s an essential part of the development process, and there are several tools and techniques you can use to effectively debug your Vue code.

topics.description: As we develop our applications we may need to communicate with the parent component in order to notify of some actions e.g. when a user clicks on a button. In order to do this we need to use events.

topics.name: Fetch
topics.description: The `fetch()` method in JavaScript is used to request to the server and load the information on the webpages. The request can be of any APIs that return the data of the format JSON or XML. This method returns a promise.

topics.description: Modifiers are special suffixes that can be added to directives (such as v-model and v-bind) to alter their behavior. Modifiers allow you to specify additional functionality or constraints to the binding, making it more flexible and powerful.

topics.name: Templates

topics.name: V Else If
topics.description: This directive is used to add additional conditions to a v-if and v-else block.

topics.description: The `v-else` conditionally renders an element or a template fragment as a function in case the `v-if` does not fulfil the condition.

topics.name: V On
topics.description: The v-on directive is placed on an element to attach an event listener. To attach an event listener with v-on we need to provide the event type, and any modifier, and a method or expression that should run when that event occurs.

topics.name: V Once
topics.description: The `v-once` directive makes an HTML element render only once, skipping every future update.

topics.name: V Pre
topics.description: The `v-pre` directive makes an element render its content as-is, skipping its compilation. The most common use case is when displaying raw mustache syntax.

topics.name: V Slot
topics.description: The v-slot directive to define slots in components, allowing you to pass and render content dynamically inside a component.
